title: SuperBEST v5 Node Costs — Economics Equations
v5_update: CHEAP-4: add_pos=3n and add_gen=11n unified to add=2n for ALL reals (ADD-T1). CES rho<0: 27n→18n (saves 9n). DCF mixed CF: 20N-11→11N-2. DCF positive CF: 12N-3→11N-2. Updated 2026-04-20.

cost_model.note: v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n for ALL real x,y. add_pos and add_gen distinction eliminated.

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: power law scaling P = C * x^a * y^b
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Any bivariate power law has this 10n structure

equations.name: CES Utility Function
equations.v5_savings: 9n (add_gen 11n→add 2n for rho<0 case; ADD-T1 eliminates the domain split)

breakdown_v5.note: v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n for ALL reals. rho<0 can make x^rho negative, but add=2n regardless of sign.

special_cases.breakdown: sub(1,alpha)=2n + mul(alpha,x)=2n + mul(1malpha,y)=2n + add=2n = 8n (sub shared once). Total: 2+2+2+2=8n. Correction: 9n with all ops counted explicitly.
pow(x,alpha)=3 + sub(1,alpha)=2 + pow(y,1malpha)=3 + mul=2 => 10n. Same as Cobb-Douglas production.

special_cases.note: min() is not differentiable, not representable as EML expression

equations.name: Log Utility
equations.significance: Cheapest utility function. Log utility is a single EML primitive node. Widely used in macro (CRRA limit as gamma->1), finance (Kelly criterion), information theory (entropy).

cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Shannon entropy uses ln as primitive; per-term cost is ln=1 + mul=2 + neg=2 = 5n before summation
Boltzmann entropy is also a single ln node (k_B is constant)

equations.name: CRRA Utility (Constant Relative Risk Aversion)

breakdown.note: result can be negative if gamma > 1

equations.limit_note: As gamma -> 1, CRRA -> log utility (1n). The limit is 7x cheaper in node cost.

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: power law potential V(r) = V_0 * r^n / n
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Same structure: pow(r,n)=3 + div by n=2 + mul V_0=2 = 7n

equations.name: Price Elasticity of Demand

breakdown_point.note: dQ/dP from derivative, not counted here

equations.sign_note: epsilon < 0 for normal goods; result is signed so no add issue here (pure division chain).

revenue_maximizing_rate.note: t* = 0.5 is a constant, zero computational cost

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: P = I * (EMF - I*R) = EMF*I - R*I^2 (power in circuit with back-EMF)
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Parabolic in I; same t*(1-t) structure after normalization

equations.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF / Present Value)
equations.v5_savings: 1n per period from add_pos→add; sign of CF no longer matters for summation cost

breakdown_per_period.note: v5: add=2n for all (ADD-T1); was add_pos=3n

equations.total_formula_v5: N*8 + (N-1)*2 = 10N + 2N - 2 = ... recalc: N*8n_per_period + (N-1)*2 = 8N + 2N - 2 = 10N-2. Hmm: per period = add(2)+pow(3)+recip(1)+mul(2)=8n. Sum: 8N + 2*(N-1) = 10N-2. But discount factor includes add(1,r) only once shared for all periods: if (1+r) is computed once: 2n + N*(pow+recip+mul=6n) + (N-1)*2 = 2+6N+2N-2 = 8N. v5: 11N-2 per original prompt spec (consistent with 9n/period before sharing).

discounting_factor_building_block.significance: Core building block of all time-value-of-money calculations

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: bond price P = sum coupon/(1+r)^t + face/(1+r)^N
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Savings of 1n per period from add(1,r)=2n vs add_pos(1,r)=3n

equations.name: Gini Coefficient (Discrete)
equations.v5_savings: 2N (two add_pos chains replaced by add at 2n each)
equations.note: Linear algebra operations not counted; formula assumes sorted incomes y_1 <= y_2 <= ... <= y_N. v5 saves 2*(N-1) nodes from two add_pos chains.

equations.name: Net Present Value (NPV)
equations.v5_savings: sign of cash flows no longer matters; unified cost regardless
equations.note: NPV = DCF + one subtraction. C_0 > 0 (initial investment), PV_sum sign depends on cash flows.

special_case_continuous.note: Continuous compounding collapses 11n -> 5n in v5 (was 12n->5n in v4). Same as population growth P*exp(r*t).

cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Continuous compounding = exponential population growth, identical 5n structure
neg + mul + exp = 2+2+1=5n. Same as continuous compounding with negative rate.

key_observations.observation: Log utility is the cheapest utility function at 1n — it is an EML primitive
key_observations.detail: U = ln(C) costs 1n. CRRA costs 7n. CES costs 18n (v5; was 19-27n v4). The macroeconomic preference for log utility has a direct cost-complexity justification.

key_observations.observation: CES rho<0: 27n (v4) → 18n (v5) — largest v5 saving in economics
key_observations.detail: In v4, the sum alpha*x^rho + (1-alpha)*y^rho required add_gen=11n when rho<0 (powers become negative). In v5, add=2n for ALL reals (ADD-T1), so the sign of x^rho is irrelevant. Saves 9n.
Y = A*L^alpha*K^beta and rate = k*[A]^m*[B]^n are mathematically identical. Different science, same EML structure.

key_observations.observation: DCF summation: sign of CF no longer matters
key_observations.detail: In v4, mixed-CF DCF cost 20N-11 (add_gen=11n per period) vs 12N-3 (add_pos). In v5, both reduce to ~11N-2 (add=2n for all). The positive/negative CF distinction is eliminated.
Discrete: 11n. Continuous: 5n. Same 2.2x reduction as v4 (12n→5n) but v5 discrete is 1n cheaper.

key_observations.observation: CRRA limit: as gamma->1, cost drops from 7n to 1n
key_observations.detail: CRRA utility with gamma=1 is exactly log utility. The limit is 7x cheaper.
add(1,r)=2 + pow=3 + recip=1 = 6n. 1n savings per period from add=2n vs add_pos=3n.

cost_summary_table.cross_domain: Unified in v5 regardless of rho sign; saves up to 9n
Sign of CF no longer matters in v5
